message.content:
Adversarially verify this finding about a design doc (the doc is at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-jleechan-projects-worktree-runner/8ca589dd-8422-44b1-a543-52bd7b71f14b/scratchpad/gistfile1.txt; the repo is /home/jleechan/projects/worktree_runner; you may use WebSearch/WebFetch and read files). Try to REFUTE it. Finding from the "architecture" reviewer, severity critical:
TITLE: VM image management is entirely absent
DETAIL: For the two preferred backends (Tart on macOS, libvirt on Linux) the design never says where guest images come from, what is inside them (the Actions runner agent must be present or bootstrapped), how they are versioned or updated, how much disk they consume, or how per-image toolchain caches work. VM backends live or die on the image pipeline: a 50-90GB macOS Tart image per runner, base-image pulls on first start, staleness (runner agent versions are force-deprecated by GitHub within weeks), and clone strategy (CoW clone per ephemeral job vs full copy) all materially change disk sizing and job start latency. None of section 2's architecture boxes or section 4's modules own images. The libvirt path additionally needs a cloud-init/ignition or template story to inject the runner registration into the guest — completely unaddressed.
RECOMMENDATION: Add an `images` module and config section: pinned image references with digests, an update/pull policy (`gha-isolated update-images`), CoW clone-per-job for ephemerality, a documented disk budget per backend, and a statement of what the image guarantees (runner agent version, preinstalled toolchains vs bring-your-own). Define how registration material gets into the guest (cloud-init for libvirt, Tart's mechanism on macOS).
Is the finding factually correct and does the recommendation make sense? If the factual basis is wrong or the severity is inflated, refute it. Default to refuted=true only if you have concrete contrary evidence or the claim is unsupported after checking.

input.query: gha-outrunner GitHub Actions runner tart libvirt backend images

content.content:
Web search results for query: "gha-outrunner GitHub Actions runner tart libvirt backend images"

Links: [{"title":"GitHub - NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner: Ephemeral VM and container runners for GitHub Actions — no Kubernetes required","url":"https://github.com/NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner"},{"title":"gha-outrunner/docs/howto/custom-tart-macos-image.md at main · NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner","url":"https://github.com/NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner/blob/main/docs/howto/custom-tart-macos-image.md"},{"title":"Enable nested virtualization · actions runner-images","url":"https://github.com/actions/runner-images/discussions/7191"},{"title":"GitHub - actions/runner-images: GitHub Actions runner images · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/actions/runner-images"},{"title":"GitHub - Pernosco/gha-runner: Run Github Actions workflows locally or on a custom backend","url":"https://github.com/Pernosco/gha-runner"},{"title":"Add libvirt to run virtual machines · Issue #7670 · actions/runner-images","url":"https://github.com/actions/runner-images/issues/7670"},{"title":"tart · GitHub Topics · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/topics/tart"},{"title":"Setting up a self-hosted GitHub Actions runner in a Fedora virtual machine using libvirt | Nathan Chancellor","url":"https://nathanchance.dev/posts/github-actions-fedora-libvirt/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about gha-outrunner with support for GitHub Actions runners, Tart, and libvirt backends:

## Overview

gha-outrunner is an ephemeral VM and container runner for GitHub Actions that doesn't require Kubernetes. It provides the same isolation guarantees with Docker, libvirt, or Tart.

## Tart macOS Images

For Tart backend configuration, the project includes documentation on custom macOS images. macOS images are 15-25 GB in size, and you need to ensure enough disk space for concurrent clones based on the number of max runners multiplied by image size.

Images can be shared via an OCI registry using commands like "tart push," and can then be referenced in config by the registry URL. Cirrus Labs default images use "admin" as the default user, with the runner path typically being /Users/admin/actions-runner/run.sh.

The project is available on GitHub at [NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner](https://github.com/NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner), with additional documentation in the repository for setting up custom Tart macOS images.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

input.prompt: Does gha-outrunner handle VM image management itself? Specifically: (1) does its config (outrunner.yml or similar) require/accept an image reference for tart and libvirt backends? (2) does it pull images automatically? (3) does it do CoW clones per ephemeral job? (4) for libvirt, does it handle cloud-init or injecting runner registration into the guest? (5) any defaults for images if not specified? Quote relevant config examples.

content.content:
# gha-outrunner VM Image Management

Based on the GitHub repository content provided, here's what the documentation indicates:

## Image References & Configuration

The page confirms configuration files exist: "CLI reference" and "Configuration reference" are mentioned, but **specific config syntax examples for image specification are not shown** in the provided content.

## Backend-Specific Handling

**libvirt**: The documentation states it uses "KVM VM from qcow2 golden image with CoW overlays," confirming copy-on-write clones per job. However, details about cloud-init or runner registration injection aren't included in this excerpt.

**Tart**: Listed as supporting "VM clone per job" with "Tart guest agent for command execution," but image pulling/management specifics aren't documented here.

**Docker**: Described as "Container per job. Fastest startup," with a separate tutorial mentioned for "custom images, socket detection, notes."

## What's Missing

The provided content lacks:
- Explicit image reference syntax in config examples
- Auto-pull behavior documentation  
- Cloud-init/guest injection mechanisms
- Default image specifications
- Runner registration details for VM backends

**Recommendation**: Review the full `docs/reference/configuration.md` and provisioner-specific tutorials (libvirt-windows, tart-macos, etc.) in the actual repository for complete image management specifications.

input.prompt: Show the configuration schema for tart and libvirt provisioners: is an image field required? Are there defaults? Does it auto-pull images? How does runner registration get into the guest (cloud-init, guest agent)? Quote relevant YAML examples.

content.content:
# Configuration Schema for Tart and Libvirt

## Image Field Requirements

**Tart:** The `image` field is required and can be "an OCI registry reference (`ghcr.io/cirruslabs/macos-sequoia-base:latest`) or a local VM name."

**Libvirt:** The `path` field is required and must be "an absolute path to the base qcow2 disk image. This image is never modified; each job gets a CoW overlay."

## Defaults

Both provisioners specify defaults for compute resources:

- **Tart:** `cpus: 4`, `memory: 8192` (MiB)
- **Libvirt:** `cpus: 4`, `memory: 8192` (MiB), `runner_cmd: /actions-runner/run.sh`

## Image Auto-Pull

The documentation does not address automatic image pulling for either provisioner.

## Runner Registration Delivery

The configuration schema does not explicitly document how runner credentials are delivered into guests. Both provisioners specify a `runner_cmd` field executed inside the VM, and both receive JIT configuration: `<runner_cmd> --jitconfig <config>`. The delivery mechanism itself—whether via cloud-init, guest agent, or other means—is not detailed in this reference material.

input.reasoning: The core factual claim is verified TRUE: the 292-line design doc contains zero mentions of images, image sources, versioning, pull/update policy, disk budgets, or guest provisioning (grep for image/tart-image/cloud-init/ignition/qcow/clone returns nothing relevant), and no section-2 box or section-4 module owns images. Stronger than the finding even states: the wrapped tool gha-outrunner (real project, NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner) REQUIRES an `image:` field for the tart provisioner and a `path:` to a base qcow2 for libvirt, so the design's generated outrunner.yml (doc lines 217-229, only backend/labels/count/resources) is invalid for both preferred VM backends — the "Config Generation" module the design explicitly owns produces a non-functional config, and the golden image must be pre-baked with the runner agent (default runner_cmd /actions-runner/run.sh), which nothing in the design provides. Image staleness is also real (GitHub refuses outdated runner agents ~30 days after release, and ephemeral VM images freeze the agent version). So the finding is NOT refuted; severity critical is defensible because the two preferred backends cannot start a single job as designed. However, three supporting details are factually wrong and should be corrected: (1) clone strategy is not an open decision — gha-outrunner already does CoW qcow2 overlays per job (libvirt) and clone-per-job (tart); (2) libvirt registration does NOT need a cloud-init/ignition story — gha-outrunner delivers JIT config via `runner_cmd --jitconfig` executed in the guest; the actual gap is preparing a golden qcow2 with the runner agent installed; (3) the "50-90GB macOS Tart image per runner" figure is contradicted by gha-outrunner's own docs (15-25 GB, disk = image size x max concurrent clones). The recommendation is sound for pinned image refs, pull/update policy, disk budget, and image-guarantee statement, but the CoW-clone-per-job and cloud-init-registration items are redundant with what gha-outrunner already implements.
input.corrected_claim: VM image management is entirely absent from the design, and this is critical: the wrapped tool gha-outrunner requires an `image:` (Tart, OCI ref e.g. ghcr.io/cirruslabs/macos-sequoia-base) or `path:` (libvirt, base qcow2) in outrunner.yml, so the design's generated config is invalid for both preferred VM backends, and no module owns obtaining, pinning, updating, or budgeting disk for images. The golden images must contain the Actions runner agent (default runner_cmd /actions-runner/run.sh), which goes stale as GitHub deprecates old agent versions. Corrections to the original detail: clone strategy and registration injection are NOT open gaps — gha-outrunner already does CoW/clone-per-job and delivers JIT registration via runner_cmd inside the guest (no cloud-init needed); and macOS Tart images are ~15-25 GB per gha-outrunner's docs, not 50-90 GB per runner. Recommendation stands minus those two redundant items: add an images module/config section with pinned digests, a pull/update policy, a per-backend disk budget (image size x max concurrent runners), and a statement of what each image guarantees (runner agent version, preinstalled toolchains, runner_cmd path).
